title: Getting Started
text: AdherenceFromOMOP relies CDMConnector database connection options currently unavailable.
code: devtools::install_github("https://github.com/HealthInformaticsUT/AdherenceFromOMOP/") readRenviron("~/.Renviron")  writePrefix <- "adherencefromomop_"  db <- DBI::dbConnect(   RPostgres::Postgres(),   dbname = Sys.getenv("DB_NAME"),   host = Sys.getenv("DB_HOST"),   user = Sys.getenv("DB_USERNAME"),   password = Sys.getenv("DB_PASSWORD"),   port = Sys.getenv("DB_PORT") )  cdm <- CDMConnector::cdmFromCon(   con = db,   cdmSchema = Sys.getenv("OHDSI_CDM"),   writeSchema = Sys.getenv("OHDSI_WRITE"),   writePrefix = writePrefix )

title: Getting Started
text: Using adherenceFromOMOP involves two main steps. First, required data must retrieved written designated table database. extract necessary data, use generateChronicDrugExposure. drugConceptIdList input variable, expects drug_concept_id values list, recommended use CodelistGenerator. Medication groups can defined calculate adherence therapeutic category. , reuse earlier drugIngredientCodes object. compute adherence, large datasets use loader functions.
code: # Step 0 - cdm connection # for demos mock dataset is recommended library(AdherenceFromOMOP) cdm <- mockAdherenceFromOMOP() # recommended method for acquiring drug_concept_id values drugIngredientCodes <- CodelistGenerator::getDrugIngredientCodes(cdm = cdm, name = c(42899580, 37498042)) concepts_ids <- unlist(drugIngredientCodes, use.names = F)  # Step 1 - data generation chronicDrugExposure <- generateChronicDrugExposure(   cdm = cdm,   # drugConceptIdList = concepts_ids,   name = "drug_exposure_demo",   overwrite = T )  chronicDrugExposure adherence <- adherenceCalculationsLoader(   reference = chronicDrugExposure,   cdm = cdm,   medicationGroup = drugIngredientCodes,   resultsTableName = "basic_adherence",   cma = c(     "CMA3"   ) )

code: # demo data shows only NA for avg_bmi cma_with_bmi <- addBMI(adherence, cdm)

title: Getting Started
code: pathToCohorts <- paste0(getwd(),"/inst/") # path to cohorts   cohortSet <- CDMConnector::readCohortSet(pathToCohorts) cdm <- CDMConnector::generateCohortSet(cdm, cohortSet, name = "cohort")  cohort <- dplyr::collect(cdm$cohort)  adherence_with_cohorts_as_features <- addCohorts(adherence, cohort, cohortIdMappingToNames = cohortSet)

code: stats <- countPeople(cdm = cdm, generatedTable = chronicDrugExposure, cmaTable = adherence)

title: Data generation
text: useful restrict adherence calculations individuals belong specific cohort. AdherenceFromOMOP supports directly cohortSchema cohortId arguments generateChronicDrugExposure() drug exposure events satisfy following conditions included: person_id included cohort, drug_exposure_start_date falls within cohort start–end period
code: # preparation, no need to replicate cdm <- mockAdherenceFromOMOP()  cohort <- dplyr::tibble(   cohort_definition_id = 1, subject_id = 1,   cohort_start_date = as.Date("1980-01-01"),   cohort_end_date = as.Date("2020-01-10") )  DBI::dbWriteTable(conn = CDMConnector::cdmCon(cdm), name = DBI::Id(schema = "main", table = "cohort"), value = cohort, append = TRUE) chronicDrugExposureCohort <- generateChronicDrugExposure(   cdm = cdm,   name =  "test_table",   overwrite = T,   cohortSchema = "main",   cohortId = 1, )

title: Data generation
text: may optionally restrict included drug exposure events specific list drug_concept_id values using drugConceptIdList argument. list can generated manually , conveniently, CodelistGenerator::getDrugIngredientCodes().
code: ingredients <- c(...) medicationGroups <- CodelistGenerator::getDrugIngredientCodes(cdm = cdm,                                                       name = ingredients,                                                       nameStyle = "{concept_code}")  ingredientNames <- names(medicationGroups) drug_concept_id_lst <- unlist(medicationGroups, use.names = F)

title: Calculating Adherence
text: Medication adherence calculations depend CMA (Continuous Medication Availability) measure selected. AdherenceFromOMOP supports CMA types 1–9. examples illustrating behavior different CMA types, see Intro CMA tutorial. package adherenceSlidingWindow adherenceCalculations wrappers functions AdhereR package. detailed guidance, refer AdhereR documentation. large datasets, recommended use adherenceSlidingWindowLoader adherenceCalculationsLoader, load data chunks write results directly database. AdhereR software follow-window total period relevant medication events recorded patients, observation window period within follow-window adherence actually computed. Within OMOP framework, adherence can computed two ways, depending whether cohorts used data generation: cohorts present: Cohort start/end dates define AdhereR observation window. Observation period start/end dates define AdhereR follow-window. cohorts used: observation period start/end dates define follow-observation windows.

title: Calculating Adherence
text: default, CMA values calculated using medication data available input dataset. compute adherence specific drug groups separate medications categories, medicationGroups argument can supplied. argument expects named list. recommended generate lists using CodelistGenerator package. medications overlap across groups, CMA values unaffected (data omitted). details, see medication.groups documentation AdhereR. example manually creating medication groups:
code: medicationGroup <- list(   c(1361364),   c(1830280)   # vectors of drug_concept_ids ) groupNames <- c("group1", "group2")  names(medicationGroup) <- groupNames  # Optional: wrap for improved display medicationGroup <- omopgenerics::newCodelist(medicationGroup)

title: Calculating Adherence
text: used calculating adherence throughout subjects observation period.
code: results <- adherenceCalculationsLoader(chronicDrugExposure, cdm, resultsTableName = "AdherenceFromOMOP_demo_cma5", cma = c("CMA5"), medicationGroup = medicationGroup) results

title: Calculating Adherence
text: approach computes adherence series sliding windows within observation period, allowing adherence examined time. Unlike AdhereR function output, window.ID values unique across records given patient. assigned sequentially within contiguous period without NA values reset gaps. Setting delayObservationWindowStart = TRUE delays start observation period used adherence calculations, case sliding-window sequence first prescription dispensed.
code: results <- adherenceSlidingWindowLoader(chronicDrugExposure, cdm, resultsTableName = "AdherenceFromOMOP_demo_sw", cma = c("CMA5"), delayObservationWindowStart = FALSE, medicationGroup = medicationGroup) results results_delayed <- adherenceSlidingWindowLoader(   chronicDrugExposure,   cdm,   resultsTableName = "AdherenceFromOMOP_demo_sw_delayed",   cma = c("CMA5"),   delayObservationWindowStart = TRUE,   sliding.window.duration = 1,   sliding.window.duration.unit = c("days", "weeks", "months", "years")[4],   sliding.window.step.duration = 1,   sliding.window.step.unit = c("days", "weeks", "months", "years")[4] ) results_delayed

title: Intro to CMA
text: Medication availability based days supplied (excluding last event) Events start observation window included Requires ≥2 events Computes: sum(days_supply values except last) / (days first last event)

title: Intro to CMA
text: Medication availability including last event, using observation window end Events start observation window included Computes: sum(days_supply values) / (time first event → observation window end)

title: Intro to CMA
text: formulas CMA1 CMA2, capped 100%.

title: Intro to CMA
text: Proportion gap days relative time first last event. Equivalent 1 – CMA1 computed using theoretical use accumulated gap time. Requires ≥2 events Events start observation window included Computes: (number days theoretical use) / (days first last event) Theoretical use sample patient number 1: 30 + 90 + 90. start last event cuts length second last event. CMA1 second last event included entirely.

title: Intro to CMA
text: Proportion gap days relative time first end observation window. Equivalent 1 – CMA2 computed using theoretical use accumulated gap time. Events start observation window included Theoretical use sample patient number 1: 30 + 90 + 90 + 36.

title: Intro to CMA
text: Gap-based measure using full observation window start → end. anchoring first event. measures adherence relative entire observation period. Includes events observation period starts Theoretical use sample patient number 1: 25 + 30 + 90 + 90 + 36.

title: Intro to CMA
text: Lagged version CMA7. observation window starts short “lag” event starts observation window. Useful excluding initial adjustment period. Computes: (number days theoretical use) / (lagged start end observation window) Number days accounting lagged start patient 1: 481 - 25.

title: Intro to CMA
text: Weighted daily adherence measure. method evaluates, every day, much medication supply covers day averages across entire window. Useful event timing overlaps matter substantially. Computes (number days observation window, weighted ratio days supply applicable event interval) / (start end observation window) Patient 3: ((1 - 612/702) * 306) / 481 612 number gap days end first () event end follow-window. 702 number days start first event end follow-window. 306 number days start first event end observation window. 481 total length observation window. Patient 2: ((1 - 0/30)30 + (1 - 60/180)180 + (1 - 492/522) * 126) / 481 Patient 1: ((1 - 120/180)145 + (1 - 0/30)30 + (1 - 90/180)180 + (1 - 0/90) 90 + (1 - 162/432) * 36) / 481

title: Example use cases with statins
code: readRenviron("~/.Renviron")  writePrefix <- "adherencefromomop_"  db <- DBI::dbConnect(   RPostgres::Postgres(),   dbname = Sys.getenv("DB_NAME"),   host = Sys.getenv("DB_HOST"),   user = Sys.getenv("DB_USERNAME"),   password = Sys.getenv("DB_PASSWORD"),   port = Sys.getenv("DB_PORT") )  cdm <- CDMConnector::cdmFromCon(   con = db,   cdmSchema = Sys.getenv("OHDSI_CDM"),   writeSchema = Sys.getenv("OHDSI_WRITE"),   writePrefix = writePrefix ) statiinid <- c("Atorvastatin", "Rosuvastatin", "Simvastatin", "Pravastatin", "Fluvastatin", "Lovastatin", "Pitavastatin")

title: Example use cases with statins
text: calculate adherence medication group groups must defined first:
code: medicationGroups <- CodelistGenerator::getDrugIngredientCodes(cdm = cdm,                                                       name = statiinid,                                                       nameStyle = "{concept_code}")  ingredientNames <- names(medicationGroups) concepts <- unlist(medicationGroups, use.names = F) # generate chronic drug exposure uses codelist generator for finding suitable drugs by default ingredients (provided by drugConceptList) are included 1 out of inf.  all_statins <- generateChronicDrugExposure(   cdm = cdm,   drugConceptIdList = concepts,   name = "statins_study",   overwrite = T )  # if the table already exists in database all_statins <- dplyr::tbl(db, CDMConnector::inSchema(CDMConnector::cdmWriteSchema(cdm), table = "statins_study", CDMConnector::dbms(db))) adherenceCMA5 <- adherenceCalculationsLoader(   reference = all_statins,   cdm = cdm,   medicationGroup = medicationGroups,   resultsTableName = "statins_cma5",   cma = "CMA5" )  # if the table already exists in database adherenceCMA5 <- dplyr::tbl(db, CDMConnector::inSchema(CDMConnector::cdmWriteSchema(cdm), table = "statins_cma5", CDMConnector::dbms(db)))

what: Adherence for each statin group using cohorts
title: Example use cases with statins
text: selecting people specific cohorts cohort id cohort schema values must used.
code: all_statins_cohort <- generateChronicDrugExposure(   cdm = cdm,   drugConceptList = statiinid,   name = "statins_study_1688",   overwrite = T,   cohortId = 1688,   cohortSchema = "ohdsi_results_202503" )  # if the table already exists in database  all_statins_cohort <- dplyr::tbl(db, CDMConnector::inSchema(CDMConnector::cdmWriteSchema(cdm), table = "statins_study_1688", CDMConnector::dbms(db)))

previous_headings: Example use cases with statins > Adherence for each statin group using cohorts
what: Combining medication groups for simvastatin and rosuvastatin
title: Example use cases with statins
code: medicationGroups <- CodelistGenerator::getDrugIngredientCodes(   cdm = cdm,   name = c("Atorvastatin", "Rosuvastatin", "Simvastatin", "Pravastatin", "Fluvastatin", "Lovastatin", "Pitavastatin"),   nameStyle = "{concept_name}" )  medicationGroups[["simvastatin_rosuvastatin"]] <- c(medicationGroups[["simvastatin"]], medicationGroups[["rosuvastatin"]])  medicationGroups[["simvastatin"]] <- NULL medicationGroups[["rosuvastatin"]] <- NULL  names(medicationGroups) adherenceCMA5 <- adherenceCalculationsLoader(   reference = all_statins_cohort,   cdm = cdm,   medicationGroup = medicationGroups,   resultsTableName = "statins_study_1688_medgroups",   cma = "CMA5" ) adherenceCMA5SW <- adherenceSlidingWindowLoader(   reference = all_statins_cohort,   cdm = cdm,   medicationGroup = medicationGroups,   resultsTableName = "statins_study_1688_medgroups_sw",   cma = "CMA5",   sliding.window.start = 0,   sliding.window.start.unit = c("days", "weeks", "months", "years")[1],   sliding.window.duration = 3,   sliding.window.duration.unit = c("days", "weeks", "months", "years")[4],   sliding.window.step.duration = 3,   sliding.window.step.unit = c("days", "weeks", "months", "years")[4], )

title: Example use cases with statins
text: Adding cohorts features CMA data requires cohort table input variable. recommended use CDMConnector generating . Adding body mass index separate function. get overview function reports number individuals three groups: (1) individuals included cohort, individuals calculated adherence values, individuals least n drug exposure records within given calendar year, calculated generated drug exposure table.
code: cohortSet <- CDMConnector::readCohortSet("./inst") cdm <- CDMConnector::generateCohortSet(cdm, cohortSet, "cohort")  cohortTable <- dplyr::collect(cdm$cohort)  cma_values_with_cohort_features <- addCohort(adherenceCMA5, cohortTable) cma_data_with_bmi <- addBMI(adherenceCMA5, cdm) stats <- countPeople(cdm = cdm,                         cmaTable = adherenceCMA5,                         generatedTable = all_statins)

title: Calculate medication adherence using data from OMOP CDM
text: Adherence concept indicates well patient follow healthcare provider’s recommendation. Medication adherence specifically shows patient follows medication regimen gaps. important medications meant consumed chronic diseases, efficacy treatment closely correlated continuity medication consumption. One possibility measure adherence secondary database analysis. AdherenceFromOMOP contains functions measure chronic medication adherence using drug_exposure table data mapped OMOP Common Data Model. package supports: generating drug exposure records cohorts /specific medications calculating medication adherence observation period calculating medication adherence using sliding window approach view specific periods observation period add cohort features columns data returned adherence functions view number people areas interest

title: Calculate medication adherence using data from OMOP CDM
code: devtools::install_github("https://github.com/HealthInformaticsUT/AdherenceFromOMOP/") library(AdherenceFromOMOP)

title: Calculate medication adherence using data from OMOP CDM
text: AdherenceFromOMOP relies CDMConnector database connection options currently unavailable.
code: readRenviron("~/.Renviron")  writePrefix <- "adherencefromomop_"  db <- DBI::dbConnect(   RPostgres::Postgres(),   dbname = Sys.getenv("DB_NAME"),   host = Sys.getenv("DB_HOST"),   user = Sys.getenv("DB_USERNAME"),   password = Sys.getenv("DB_PASSWORD"),   port = Sys.getenv("DB_PORT") )  cdm <- CDMConnector::cdmFromCon(   con = db,   cdmSchema = Sys.getenv("OHDSI_CDM"),   writeSchema = Sys.getenv("OHDSI_WRITE"),   writePrefix = writePrefix )

title: Calculate medication adherence using data from OMOP CDM
text: Using adherenceFromOMOP involves two main steps. First, required data must retrieved written designated table database. extract necessary data, use generateChronicDrugExposure. drugConceptIdList input variable, expects drug_concept_id values list, recommended use CodelistGenerator.
code: # recommended method for acquiring drug_concept_id values drugIngredientCodes <- CodelistGenerator::getDrugIngredientCodes(cdm = cdm, name = c("Atorvastatin")) concepts_ids <- unlist(drugIngredientCodes, use.names = F)  # Step 1 - data generation chronicDrugExposure <- generateChronicDrugExposure(   cdm = cdm,   drugConceptIdList = concepts_ids,   name = "chronic_drug_exposure_table",   overwrite = T )  chronicDrugExposure adherence <- adherenceCalculations(data = chronicDrugExposure, cdm, cma = c("CMA5"))

what: Add BMI category to CMA results — addBMI
title: Add BMI category to CMA results — addBMI
text: Enriches CMA calculation results Body Mass Index (BMI) categories derived measurement records OMOP CDM. sliding window calculations, BMI matched measurements taken within one year window start date. simple CMA calculations, average BMI across measurements used. BMI concept_id 4245997 used identify BMI measurements.

title: Add BMI category to CMA results — addBMI
code: addBMI(cma_table, cdm)

title: Add BMI category to CMA results — addBMI
text: cma_table Data frame database table reference containing CMA results. Must include person_id column. sliding window results, also contain window.start window.end columns. cdm cdm_reference object created CDMConnector, providing access measurement table containing BMI records.

title: Add BMI category to CMA results — addBMI
text: Data frame original CMA columns plus bmi_category column containing   one : "normal weight" (BMI < 25), "Overweight" (25 <= BMI < 30),   "Obese" (BMI >= 30), "Unknown" (BMI measurement available).   sliding window results, also includes measurement_date.

title: Add BMI category to CMA results — addBMI
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cdm <- mockAdherenceFromOMOP() cma_results <- adherenceSlidingWindow(cdm, name = "drug_exposure", cma = "CMA5") cma_with_bmi <- addBMI(cma_results, cdm) } # }

what: Add cohort membership indicators to CMA results — addCohorts
title: Add cohort membership indicators to CMA results — addCohorts
text: Enriches CMA calculation results cohort membership information joining CMA data cohort tables. cohort, adds binary indicator columns (whether patient cohort observation window) time--cohort columns (number days patient spent cohort window). Handles sliding window simple CMA results.

title: Add cohort membership indicators to CMA results — addCohorts
code: addCohorts(cmaTable, cohortTable, cohortIdMappingToNames = NULL)

title: Add cohort membership indicators to CMA results — addCohorts
text: cmaTable Data frame database table reference containing CMA results. Must include person_id column. sliding window results, contain window.start window.end columns; simple CMA, contain observation_window_start observation_period_end_date. cohortTable Data frame database table reference containing cohort data columns: cohort_definition_id, subject_id, cohort_start_date, cohort_end_date. cohortIdMappingToNames Optional named list mapping cohort IDs readable names output columns. contain elements "cohort_name" "cohort_definition_id" parallel vectors. NULL, cohort IDs used column name suffixes.

title: Add cohort membership indicators to CMA results — addCohorts
text: Data frame original CMA columns plus, cohort: binary   indicator column (cohort_name) time--cohort column (time_in_name)   showing days overlap cohort period CMA observation window.   Returns NULL cohort periods overlap CMA calculation periods.

title: Add cohort membership indicators to CMA results — addCohorts
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cdm <- mockAdherenceFromOMOP() cma_results <- adherenceCalculations(cdm, name = "drug_exposure", cma = "CMA5") cohort_mapping <- list(cohort_name = c("diabetes", "hypertension"),                        cohort_definition_id = c(1, 2)) cma_with_cohorts <- addCohorts(cma_results, cdm$cohort, cohort_mapping) } # }

what: Add patient information to CMA results — addPatientInformation
title: Add patient information to CMA results — addPatientInformation
text: Enriches CMA calculation results patient demographic clinical information original drug exposure data. sliding window calculations, patient information matched based drug exposures falling within window period. rows drug exposure exists within window, patient information (except time-dependent fields like days_to_death) filled rows person.

title: Add patient information to CMA results — addPatientInformation
code: addPatientInformation(data, cma_values)

title: Add patient information to CMA results — addPatientInformation
text: data Data frame containing drug exposure records patient information, typically output generateChronicDrugExposure. Must contain person_id, drug_exposure_start_date, days_supply, drug_concept_id, patient info columns. cma_values Data frame containing CMA calculation results AdhereR functions. sliding window results, contain window.start window.end columns.

title: Add patient information to CMA results — addPatientInformation
text: Data frame combining CMA results patient information. sliding window   results, window row matched patient info drug exposures within   window. days_to_death column NA windows without drug exposures.

what: Calculating simple CMAs for the duration on observation period — adherenceCalculations
title: Calculating simple CMAs for the duration on observation period — adherenceCalculations
text: Calculating simple CMAs duration observation period

title: Calculating simple CMAs for the duration on observation period — adherenceCalculations
code: adherenceCalculations(   cdm = NULL,   data = NULL,   cma = c("CMA1", "CMA2", "CMA3", "CMA4", "CMA5", "CMA6", "CMA7", "CMA8", "CMA9"),   name = NULL,   medicationGroup = NULL,   delayObservationWindowStart = FALSE,   cleanRows = TRUE,   ... )

title: Calculating simple CMAs for the duration on observation period — adherenceCalculations
text: cdm cdm_reference object 'name' table data instead cdm provide -memory data. cma default function calculates adherence different CMA algorithms (CMA1-CMA9). name name table used adherence calculations medicationGroup CMA calculated defined group. Medication group named list name drug_concept_id values. creating list highly recommended use CodelistGenerator "https://darwin-eu.github.io/CodelistGenerator/articles/a04_GenerateVocabularyBasedCodelist.html" delayObservationWindowStart delays adherence calculation window first medication purchase cleanRows TRUE, rows NA CMA values dropped window.ID reset sequential within contiguous non-NA CMA interval. ... input values adhereR CMA function call

title: Calculating simple CMAs for the duration on observation period — adherenceCalculations
text: table CMA results

title: Calculating simple CMAs for the duration on observation period — adherenceCalculations
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ connection <- DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(dbdir = CDMConnector::eunomiaDir()))  cdm <- CDMConnector::cdmFromCon(   con = connection,   cdmSchema = "main",   writeSchema = "main",   cdmName = "my_duckdb_database" )  cdm <- generateChronicDrugExposure(   cdm = cdm,   ingredients = c("acetaminophen", "metformin", "simvastatin"),   name = "test_table",   overwrite = TRUE ) adherenceCalculations(   cdm = cdm,   ingredient = c("acetaminophen"),   name = "test_table",   cma = "CMA1" ) } # }

title: For using adherence calculations function with large datasets. — adherenceCalculationsLoader

what: Remove rows with NA CMA values and reset window IDs — cleanNARows
title: Remove rows with NA CMA values and reset window IDs — cleanNARows
text: Filters rows CMA calculation returned NA resets window.ID numbering sequential within contiguous block non-NA results. handles gaps sliding window calculations windows may sufficient data CMA computation.

title: Remove rows with NA CMA values and reset window IDs — cleanNARows
code: cleanNARows(data)

title: Remove rows with NA CMA values and reset window IDs — cleanNARows
text: data Data frame containing CMA results columns: name (CMA type), person_id, group, CMA, optionally window.ID sliding window results.

title: Remove rows with NA CMA values and reset window IDs — cleanNARows
text: Data frame NA CMA rows removed. sliding window data, window.ID   reset start 1 within contiguous series non-NA values,   grouped name, person_id, group.

what: Generate summary counts of patients in adherence analysis — countPeople
title: Generate summary counts of patients in adherence analysis — countPeople
text: Provides overview patient counts different stages adherence analysis pipeline. Counts unique patients CMA results, drug exposure data, optionally specified cohorts. Also generates yearly breakdowns patients drug exposure records meeting minimum threshold.

title: Generate summary counts of patients in adherence analysis — countPeople
code: countPeople(   cdm = NULL,   cmaTable = NULL,   generatedTable = NULL,   cohortSchema = NULL,   cohortId = NULL,   n = 1 )

title: Generate summary counts of patients in adherence analysis — countPeople
text: cdm cdm_reference object created CDMConnector. Required cohortSchema cohortId provided count cohort membership. cmaTable Optional data frame database table reference containing CMA calculation results. provided, counts unique person_id values. generatedTable Optional data frame database table reference containing output generateChronicDrugExposure. provided, counts unique patients generates yearly drug exposure summaries. cohortSchema Optional character string specifying database schema containing cohort table. Used cohortId count patients cohorts. cohortId Optional numeric vector cohort_definition_id values count. Used together cohortSchema. n Integer specifying minimum number drug exposure records per year patient included yearly summary. Default 1.

title: Generate summary counts of patients in adherence analysis — countPeople
text: Named list containing: "Number people CMA generated " Count cmaTable provided "Number people included" Count generatedTable provided "Yearly drug exposure record count" Data frame year n_persons       columns showing patients meeting minimum record threshold per year "Number people cohort" Count cohort cohortSchema/cohortId provided

title: Generate summary counts of patients in adherence analysis — countPeople
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cdm <- mockAdherenceFromOMOP() chronicDrugExposure <- generateChronicDrugExposure(   cdm = cdm, name = "chronic_drug_exposure_table", overwrite = TRUE ) CMA_values <- adherenceCalculationsLoader(   reference = chronicDrugExposure, cdm = cdm, cma = "CMA5" ) counts <- countPeople(   cdm = cdm, cmaTable = CMA_values, generatedTable = chronicDrugExposure ) } # }

what: Generate drug exposure data for adherence calculations — generateChronicDrugExposure
title: Generate drug exposure data for adherence calculations — generateChronicDrugExposure
text: Extracts prepares drug exposure records OMOP CDM database use medication adherence calculations. Joins drug exposure data patient demographics (date birth, sex, days death) observation period information. Optionally filters specific drug concepts cohort membership.

title: Generate drug exposure data for adherence calculations — generateChronicDrugExposure
code: generateChronicDrugExposure(   cdm,   name = "adherenceFromOMOP",   indexDateForPatientCalculation = "drug_exposure_start_date",   overwrite = FALSE,   subjects = NULL,   drugConceptIdList = NULL,   cohortSchema = NULL,   cohortId = NULL,   cohortTableName = "cohort",   ... )

title: Generate drug exposure data for adherence calculations — generateChronicDrugExposure
text: cdm cdm_reference object created CDMConnector, providing access OMOP CDM tables including drug_exposure, person, observation_period, concept. name Character string specifying name output table CDM write schema. Default "adherenceFromOMOP". indexDateForPatientCalculation Character string specifying date column use reference calculating days_to_death via PatientProfiles::addDeathDays. Default "drug_exposure_start_date". overwrite Logical. TRUE, overwrites existing table name. FALSE table exists, returns NULL warning. Default FALSE. subjects Optional numeric vector person_id values include. NULL, subjects included. Useful testing analyzing specific patients. drugConceptIdList Optional numeric vector drug_concept_id values filter drug exposure records. NULL, drug exposures included. cohortSchema Optional character string specifying database schema containing cohort table. provided cohortId, drug exposures within cohort periods included. cohortId Optional numeric vector cohort_definition_id values filter patients. Used together cohortSchema restrict patients specific cohorts. cohortTableName Character string specifying cohort table name cohortSchema. Default "cohort". ... Additional arguments (currently unused, reserved future extensions).

title: Generate drug exposure data for adherence calculations — generateChronicDrugExposure
text: database table reference containing drug exposure records columns:   person_id, drug_concept_id, drug_exposure_start_date, days_supply, date_of_birth,   sex, days_to_death, observation_period_start_date, observation_period_end_date.   cohortSchema used, also includes cohort_start_date cohort_end_date.   Returns NULL validation fails records match criteria.

title: Generate drug exposure data for adherence calculations — generateChronicDrugExposure
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cdm <- mockAdherenceFromOMOP()  # Include all drugs chronicDrugExposure <- generateChronicDrugExposure(   cdm = cdm,   name = "chronic_drug_exposure_table",   overwrite = TRUE )  # Filter by specific drug concepts chronicDrugExposure <- generateChronicDrugExposure(   cdm = cdm,   drugConceptIdList = c(1234567, 2345678),   name = "filtered_exposure",   overwrite = TRUE ) } # }

what: Load and prepare data for adherence calculations — loadData
title: Load and prepare data for adherence calculations — loadData
text: Loads drug exposure data either CDM database table -memory data frame, validates required columns, computes observation window parameters needed AdhereR. Calculates observation_period_duration, followup_period_duration, observation_window_start based cohort dates (available) observation period dates.

title: Load and prepare data for adherence calculations — loadData
code: loadData(cdm, name, data, delayObservationWindowStart = FALSE)

title: Load and prepare data for adherence calculations — loadData
text: cdm cdm_reference object created CDMConnector. provided, data read database table specified name. Either cdm+name data must provided. name Character string specifying table name CDM write schema containing drug exposure data generated generateChronicDrugExposure. Required cdm provided. data -memory data frame tibble drug exposure records. Used cdm NULL. Must contain columns: person_id, drug_exposure_start_date, days_supply, drug_concept_id. delayObservationWindowStart Logical. TRUE, observation window starts first drug exposure date patient instead observation/cohort start date. Useful sliding window calculations avoid empty initial windows. Default FALSE.

title: Load and prepare data for adherence calculations — loadData
text: Data frame original columns plus computed columns: observation_period_duration,   followup_period_duration, observation_window_start. Returns NULL validation fails.

what: Create a mock OMOP CDM database for testing adherence calculations — mockAdherenceFromOMOP
title: Create a mock OMOP CDM database for testing adherence calculations — mockAdherenceFromOMOP
text: Generates synthetic OMOP CDM database DuckDB tables required adherence calculations: person, observation_period, drug_exposure, measurement, death, vocabulary tables. drug_exposure table populated computed days_supply values. Useful unit testing demonstrating package functionality without requiring access real patient data.

title: Create a mock OMOP CDM database for testing adherence calculations — mockAdherenceFromOMOP
code: mockAdherenceFromOMOP(nPerson = 5, recordPerson = 3, dbms = duckdb::duckdb())

title: Create a mock OMOP CDM database for testing adherence calculations — mockAdherenceFromOMOP
text: nPerson Integer specifying number synthetic patients generate. Passed omock::mockPerson. Default 5. recordPerson Integer specifying number drug exposure measurement records generate per person. Passed omock::mockDrugExposure omock::mockMeasurement. Default 3. dbms Database management system connection object. Currently DuckDB supported. Default duckdb::duckdb().

title: Create a mock OMOP CDM database for testing adherence calculations — mockAdherenceFromOMOP
text: cdm_reference object connected -memory DuckDB database containing   synthetic OMOP CDM tables. write schema set "main" prefix "adherencemock_".

title: Create a mock OMOP CDM database for testing adherence calculations — mockAdherenceFromOMOP
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cdm <- mockAdherenceFromOMOP() cdm <- mockAdherenceFromOMOP(nPerson = 10, recordPerson = 5) } # }
